reply_context.text: It's a similar issue as if a newspaper reported some atrocity committed by ICE where the only evidence was hearsay. We're inclined to believe the accusers because the accusations keep being true, and the accused party goes out of their way to make it hard for the truth to be independently verified.

posts.text: It's a similar issue as if a newspaper reported some atrocity committed by ICE where the only evidence was hearsay. We're inclined to believe the accusers because the accusations keep being true, and the accused party goes out of their way to make it hard for the truth to be independently verified.

reply_context.text:
I did read the article, and found myself rolling my eyes halfway down.

The problem isn't that newspapers are biasedly reporting accusations without evidence. The problem is that Israel created a situation in which the public is inclined to believe accusations because Israel suppresses the truth.
